feedback.text: Whilst I like where this initiative is going, I would kindly like to add to it to stop companies from disabling/destroying video games that they have already sold to customers as products. When a company sells a product (be that digital or physical), it should very clearly state whether they are merely leasing a product out or actually selling goods to the customer. As of right now, I have multiple older games which I own physical copies to, which have stopped working - flat out stopped working, because the publisher has decided to shut down the servers on their end and have not provided any real alternative to me for allowing me to keep playing the game - even if it was in a "less than ideal state". Meaning that essentially, I have purchased goods, but now, the seller has come to my house and taken back the goods - this needs to stop. We need very strict regulation on what is sold and what is "leased", because when the publishers take away your ability to play the video games (which you have purchased), then you never really "owned" the product. (it was never really sold, merely leased)
